transcript.message: Hello, it's wonderful to connect with you today. My name is Rohan, and I'm a recruiter with a growing fintech company. I came across your profile and was impressed with your experience in AI engineering, so I'm excited to learn more about your background. Can you start by telling me a bit about your current role and what you've been working on lately?

I'm Rajesh Ghosh, nice to speak with you, Rohan. Currently, I'm working as an HR Manager at Dunder Mifflin, which is a paper company, and my role involves more of administrative tasks rather than AI engineering. However, I do have a strong background in AI and software development, and I've been involved in various projects in the past, including working with OpenCV and Node.js.

It sounds like you have a diverse background, Rajesh, and it's great that you've had experience in AI and software development. I'm curious to know, what sparked your interest in transitioning back to a technical role, and are you actively looking for a new opportunity that aligns with your AI engineering skills?

I've been considering a transition back to a technical role for some time now, Rohan. While I've enjoyed my experience in HR, I feel that my technical skills are somewhat underutilized in my current position. I'm open to exploring opportunities that align with my AI engineering skills, but I'm also looking for a role that offers a good work-life balance and a chance to grow professionally.

It's great that you're looking for a role that aligns with your technical skills and offers a good work-life balance, Rajesh. Our company is actually looking for someone who can bring their expertise in AI engineering to help drive innovation in the fintech space. Can you tell me, what specifically excites you about the opportunity to work in fintech, and how do you think your skills could be applied to make a meaningful impact in this domain?

I'm intrigued by the fintech space, Rohan, and I think my skills in AI engineering could be applied to areas such as natural language processing, recommendation systems, and information retrieval, which could be beneficial in areas like customer service chatbots, risk analysis, and personalized financial services. I'd like to learn more about the specific projects and initiatives our company is working on in these areas.

It sounds like you have a good understanding of the potential applications of AI in fintech, Rajesh. Our company is indeed working on several projects that involve natural language processing, recommendation systems, and information retrieval. One of the key areas we're focusing on is building a hybrid search infrastructure that combines embeddings-based retrieval systems with vector databases. Can you tell me, have you had any experience with vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure in your previous roles or projects?

I've had some experience with vector databases, specifically with FAISS, which is an open-source library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. I've also worked with Hugging Face Transformers and LangChain, which involve using embeddings and vector search for various NLP tasks. However, I don't have direct experience with building a hybrid search infrastructure, but I'm familiar with the concepts and would be interested in learning more about the specific requirements and challenges of such a project.

It's great to hear that you have experience with vector databases and embeddings, Rajesh. Our company is also evaluating the performance of our search infrastructure using metrics like NDCG, MRR, and MAP, and we're looking for someone who can interpret the results and make data-driven decisions. Have you had any experience with evaluation frameworks for ranking systems, and how do you approach interpreting the results of A/B tests in a real-world setting?

I've had some experience with evaluation frameworks for ranking systems, specifically with metrics like NDCG, MRR, and MAP, through my work with OpenCV and Node.js. In my previous projects, I've used these metrics to evaluate the performance of various ranking systems and algorithms, and I'm familiar with interpreting the results to make data-driven decisions. I'd be happy to discuss more about how I approach A/B testing and interpreting results in a real-world setting.

Rajesh, it was an absolute pleasure speaking with you today and learning more about your background and experience in AI engineering. I'm impressed by your passion for the field and your willingness to explore opportunities in fintech. I'll be sure to share your profile with our hiring team, and we'll be in touch within the next few days to discuss the next steps. Thank you again for your time, and I wish you all the best in your job search.

It was great speaking with you too, Rohan, and I appreciate the time you took to discuss the opportunity with me. I'm looking forward to hearing from your hiring team and learning more about the next steps. Thank you again for your time, and I wish you and your team all the best in finding the right candidate for the role.
